steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content: Since lines $m$ and $n$ are parallel and line $\ell$ is a transversal cutting through both, the angles $x°$ and \$26°$ are on the same side of the transversal and between the parallel lines. These are co-interior angles (also called consecutive interior angles or same-side interior angles).

steps.title: Apply the co-interior angle theorem and solve
steps.content:
When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, co-interior angles are supplementary—they sum to \$180°$:

$$x + 26 = 180$$

$$x = 154$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
